atoms.source_snippet:
[package]
name = "ritual-async-fees"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
repository = "https://github.com/ritual-net/ritual-reth-internal"

[dependencies]
alloy-primitives.workspace = true
ritual-precompile-addresses.workspace = true
alloy-consensus.workspace = true
reth-ethereum-primitives.workspace = true
reth-primitives-traits.workspace = true
alloy-sol-types.workspace = true
serde_json.workspace = true
tracing.workspace = true
revm.workspace = true
hex.workspace = true
ritual-precompile-codecs.workspace = true

[dev-dependencies]
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds the `ritual-async-fees` crate manifest for async fee calculation support, with primitive, precompile address/codecs, consensus transaction, Reth primitive trait, scheduled-event parsing, JSON metadata, tracing, revm database, and hex test dependencies sourced from the workspace; the root workspace wires the crate as a member and workspace dependency.

destinations.evidence: Destination manifest preserves the same package name, version, edition, license, repository, dependency set, dependency comments, workspace dependency declarations, and empty dev-dependencies section.

Destination workspace root includes `crates/ritual-async-fees/` as a member and declares `ritual-async-fees = { path = "crates/ritual-async-fees" }` in workspace dependencies, matching the source workspace wiring.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination preserves the async fees crate's manifest and workspace visibility exactly, so downstream async-fee implementation files can resolve the same crate-local and workspace dependencies without manifest drift.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run cargo metadata or a package check for `ritual-async-fees` and assert all manifest dependencies resolve through the workspace root.
atoms.notes: This record covers only the `ritual-async-fees` manifest and root workspace member/dependency wiring; implementation files beginning with `src/constants.rs` remain for subsequent queue entries.

summary: The async fees crate manifest and root workspace wiring are exact-port preserved in the destination.
notes: Queue entry verified as line 275: A	crates/ritual-async-fees/Cargo.toml.
Source diff adds a new 39-line Cargo.toml manifest ending immediately before `crates/ritual-async-fees/src/constants.rs`.
Destination manifest and workspace member/dependency declarations preserve the source addition exactly.
No implementation files from `crates/ritual-async-fees/src/` were processed in this record.
